title: Jockeying for the KPCC president’s post gains momentum, even as uncertainty looms over the party’s Chief Minister choice
content: The frontrunners for the post of KPCC president reportedly include Benny Behanan, Kodikunnil Suresh, MP, Anto Antony, MP, and Shafi Parambil

title: Vijay, Udhayanidhi, Palaniswami take oath as MLAs in Tamil Nadu
content: AIADMK senior member C.Ve. Shanmugam (who has been holding consultations with a large group of party MLAs)  did not take oath on May 11, 2026. J.C.D. Prabhakar was nominated by the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam government for the post of the Speaker

title: Telangana BJP distances itself from POCSO case against Bandi Sanjay’s son, calls it legal issue
content: Telangana BJP president dismisses allegations of discrepancies in SIR conducted during the recent Assembly elections

title: Modi’s appeal not to buy gold and avoid foreign tours reflects inability to handle crisis: Telangana CM
content: Telangana Chief Minister alleges that that the SIR exercise was being used selectively in States where the BJP aimed to gain political advantage

title: Kerala Government formation LIVE: Impasse over chief ministerial candidate in Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul held consultations with three main contenders on May 9, 2026

title: PM austerity appeal row: BJP hits back at Rahul Gandhi; cites Jawaharlal Nehru speech
content: BJP’s IT head Amit Malviya hits back at Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi’s criticism over PM Modi’s austerity appeal amid West Asia conflict

title: Ongole-breed bulls take centre stage at national-level Bull Strength Competition in Prakasam
content: Thousands gathered at Tellapadu village in Prakasam district to watch the traditional cattle endurance event organised during a temple festival

title: BJP wades into power struggle in Congress for Kerala’s Chief Minister post
content: Mr. Chandrasekhar’s jibe at the Congress, and by extension Mr. Venugopal, came close on the heels of Prime Minister Narendra Modi alleging that “uncertainty” and the fear of factional feuding “hampered” the Congress national leadership from naming the party’s Chief Minister in Kerala with certainty, despite a historic win

title: Greatest setback for workers' rights since Independence: Congress on new labour codes
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said the Modi government waited for the Assembly elections to conclude before notifying the four "anti-worker" labour codes

title: Woman dies by suicide after fiancé’s death in bike accident
content: Prafulla reportedly expressed her wish to be buried at the same place where Mani was cremated in her suicide note

title: Karimnagar gold jewellery heist case almost cracked, key accused in armed robbery case nabbed
content: The five members of the gang reportedly stayed in private lodges in Peddapalli and Dharmapuri before committing the armed robbery

title: CBSE Class 12 results 2026: Here’s how to check your marks that are ‘coming soon’ on DigiLocker
content: Approximately 1.8 million students are reported to have appeared for the exam in the 2026 academic session across Science, Commerce and Humanities streams from February to April

title: Iltija Mufti’s X account blocked
content: It comes days after the J&K lodged a FIR against unidentified individuals “promoting separatist ideology and propagating false information, with an intent to incite unlawful activities prejudicial to the peace, sovereignty and integrity of India”

title: Former Bengal Minister Sujit Bose appears before ED in municipality recruitment scam
content: Mr. Bose reached the Enforcement Directorate (ED) office at the CGO Complex in Salt Lake around 10:30 a.m., accompanied by his son Samudra Bose

title: NEET-UG 2026: NTA says committed to fair, credible exams amid Rajasthan SOG probe
content: Seeking to reassure students and parents, the NTA said the efforts and integrity of the "very large majority of bona-fide aspirants" would not be devalued

title: 47 cases from Shivamogga district in Supreme Court eligible for settlement through mediation
content: Shivamogga DLSA member-secretary appeals to people to make use of Samadhan Samaroh and Lok Adalat to settle cases amicably

title: West Bengal Secretariat moving back to Writers’ Buildings; small traders hope for revival of businesses
content: Owners of tea stalls and small eateries, fruit vendors and traders in the surroundings of the nearly 250-year-old Writers’ Buildings said the return of government employees and visitors is expected to boost their businesses

title: Supreme Court launches ‘One Case One Data’ and ‘Su Sahay’
content: 'One Case One Data' is an initiative to streamline case management; ‘Su Sahay’ is an AI-powered chatbot for easier access to court-related services for litigants

title: IT industry body NITES seeks official advisory for Work from Home measures amid rising oil prices
content: NITES called on India’s Ministry of Labour and Employment to issue an advisory that would make work-from-home mandatory for IT workers, where possible

title: Manipur Naga bodies seek PM’s ‘personal’ intervention against armed Kuki groups aiding external aggression
content: The civil society groups said that the use of drones, rocket launchers, and other military-grade weapons, along with reports that armed cadres crossed the India-Myanmar border, pointed to a well-coordinated foreign-backed assault

title: Nitin Gadkari inaugurates barrier less Mundka-Bakkarwala toll plaza on Urban Extension Road-II
content: NHAI is planning to roll out more Multi-Lane Free Flow tolling at national highway fee plazas covering 17 fee plazas across nine states; all aimed to go live by September 2026

title: AI-enabled oversight layer for continuous electoral roll monitoring
content: SIR 2.0 exposed structural weaknesses in electoral roll revision processes; an AI-enabled oversight layer integrated with ECINet could continuously monitor electoral operations, detect anomalies and discriminatory patterns in real time, and strengthen transparency, neutrality, and accountability

title: With several files ‘awaiting nod from Centre’, J&K CM Omar meets Home Minister Amit Shah
content: Earlier in the day, the J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah backed the Centre’s decision to grant the telecom powers to Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha

title: Andhra High Court judge-young advocate incident ‘amicably resolved’: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court directed grievance redressal committees to be formed in High Courts and lower courts across the country to resolve issues between the Bar and the Bench

title: Mahesh Narayanan on ‘Patriot’: I didn’t have to make compromises as a filmmaker because of the star power of Mammootty and Mohanlal
content: The Malayalam director-editor-writer opens up on what went into the making of ‘Patriot’, headlined by Mammootty, criticisms over Mohanlal’s cameo and placing substance above style

title: College lecturer duped of ₹14 lakh in fake investment scam in Karnataka
content: According to the complaint, the 52-year-old victim stated that some strangers had included his name in a Telegram group

title: Vinesh reaches Gonda, insists she is eligible to compete; WFI says her reply incomplete
content: In her response, Vinesh argued that she had formally intimated UWW about her comeback well in advance and, therefore, fulfilled the procedural requirements needed to return to competition.

title: Consensual relationship over two years not rape, says Thane court, acquits man
content: The court also observed that the survivor's evidence regarding criminal intimidation and intentional insult was "absolutely vague" and lacked "certainty and conviction"

title: VB-G RAM G Act to come into force from July 1, MGNREGA to be repealed, says Government
content: The Ministry of Rural Development assured that the transition from MGNREGA to the new framework would be seamless and without disruption to workers

title: Karnataka seeks 4 tmcft drinking water from Maharashtra
content: Cites delayed rainfall and leakage from Hippargi dam for lack of sufficient water in the six districts of Kittur Karnataka

title: If it's wrong to impose liquor ban on Hindus, how did Gujarat and Bihar do it: Iltija to Jammu and Kashmir CM
content: She was commenting on Mr. Abdullah's statement that liquor shops were to serve people of those faiths in which the consumption of alcohol was not forbidden

title: Suvendu Adhikari Cabinet approves BSF land transfer for border fencing, initiates Census exercise in Bengal
content: Alleging that the previous government did not implement the BNS in violation of the Constitution, Mr. Adhikari said the Cabinet has decided to implement the criminal law in the State

title: ‘Gracias El Clasico’: Title look of Tovino Thomas-Nazriya Nazim starrer unveiled
content: The leading duo unveiled the title look of the film during the kickoff of the El Clasico match-up between Barcelona FC and Real Madrid, streaming it live through their social media handles at 12.30 am.

title: Team SERSA wins 2026 DRM Cup Table Tennis title at Railway Indoor Stadium in Visakhapatnam
content: Team SPINX finished as runners-up and Pimples Panters third in the two-day tournament that drew 51 players from 17 teams

title: Vizianagaram women, Bapatla men win 13th State-level Beach Kabaddi Championship in Nellore
content: Teams from 25 districts competed in the four-day tournament at Duvvuru village in Atmakur constituency

title: Tamil Nadu government formation updates: AMMK chief Dhinakaran refutes party's support to TVK
content: Dhinakaran accused the TVK of submitting a "forged" letter to the Governor and slammed them for horse trading; TVK has reportedly denied these charges and accused Mr. Dhinakaran of spreading "false news"

title: Government to decide whether schools teaching religion are charitable or religious bodies: Supreme Court
content: A Bench headed by Justice Dipankar Datta said the issue is within the domain of the Ministry of Education, and the court need not intervene for now

title: Husband held for alleged dowry harassment in Visakhapatnam; wife dies 45 days after marriage
content: Police have arrested the husband based on a complaint filed by the woman’s parents alleging dowry harassment

title: PM Modi urges citizens to cut fuel use, avoid foreign travel
content: Mr. Modi suggests reviving some of the practices adopted during the COVID-19 pandemic, including work from home arrangements, online conferences and virtual meetings.

title: Who is a Pro-tem Speaker, and why is the role crucial for a new Assembly?
content: The Pro-tem Speaker serves as the essential bridge of a new Assembly, anchoring the legislative process by administering oaths and steering the House toward the election of its permanent Speaker

title: Bengal government okays land transfer to BSF for fencing Bangladesh border: CM after 1st Cabinet meeting
content: Alleging that the previous government did not implement the BNS in violation of the Constitution, he said the Cabinet decided to implement the criminal law in the State

title: Conviction of GHCAA president: Supreme Court keeps in abeyance Gujarat HC order
content: The High Court had also imposed a fine of ₹2,000 on Oza and said that in case of default, he would have to undergo simple imprisonment for two months. Mr. Oza was elected as the GHCAA president for the 19th time on December 20, 2025

title: Supreme Court dismisses PIL challenging women’s right to divorce over non-cohabitation under Hindu law
content: During the hearing, the CJI questioned the petitioner on how he was personally affected by the provision

title: Why a ministerial vacancy nearly delayed Class 12 Board exam results in T.N.
content: This year, with no School Education Minister holding the fort, the department initially decided to delay the release of the results. Owing to backlash, the results were later released as planned on May 8

title: Liquor manufacturers seek hike in prices citing West Asia crisis
content: Glass bottles, the primary packaging format for spirits, have been severely impacted owing to the energy-intensive nature of glass manufacturing; prices of plastic caps have gone up

title: Amaravati will mirror Singapore’s Punggol: Lokesh eyes World Bank push for capital project
content: The minister sought World Bank support for the Amaravati Economic Region and pitched Andhra Pradesh as a future MICE destination

title: Over 250 farm scientists meet to evolve national road map for increasing yield and area of pulses
content: Low yield is a cause for concern as pulses also play a crucial role protecting the soil health through nitrogen fixing

title: Centre extends provisions of five State legislations to Chandigarh
content: The move is part of a set of governance and regulatory reforms which are aimed at modernising legal frameworks, enhancing transparency and improving ease of living and ease of doing business.

title: The emerging Rahul Gandhi-Vijay political partnership
content: If the TVK Government were to face a challenge from the Union Government, TVK president C. Joseph Vijay could find support from Congress

title: PM Modi's appeal on fuel savings an admission of failure, will rattle markets: Akhilesh
content: Akhilesh Yadav questioned how the country would achieve the goal of becoming a "five trillion dollar economy" if the government was forced to impose several restrictions

title: NHAI plans Wayside Amenities on NH-48 in Karnataka to tackle driver fatigue, improve safety
content: These facilities are planned along strategic sections of NH 48, including the Belagavi–Kolhapur, Tumakuru–Chitradurga and Chitradurga–Hubli corridors.

title: 'We'll make a decision': Mumbai Indians provide big update on Hardik Pandya
content: Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya is sidelined with a back issue, missing consecutive IPL 2026 matches. Head coach Mahela Jayawardene confirmed the ongoing treatment, stating his return is day-to-day. This setback comes as MI, already eliminated from playoff contention, suffered another defeat, highlighting their disappointing season.

title: SC asks Mamata to file fresh plea on SIR deletions exceeding Bengal victory margins
content: The SIR of electoral rolls became one of the most fiercely debated issues in the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, with the exercise significantly reshaping the state’s voter base before polling. Nearly 91 lakh names, around 12 per cent of the electorate, were removed during the revision process. Of these, more than 60 lakh voters were marked as deceased, while nearly 27 lakh cases remained pending or under scrutiny.

title: Meta to lay off 8,000 employees this month, CEO Mark Zuckerberg's clear message for staff
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Why PM Modi wants Indians to avoid buying gold for a year
content: Prime Minister Modi urged Indians to halt gold purchases for a year to protect foreign exchange reserves, a call supported by GTRI which highlighted a surge in gold imports straining the trade balance. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw echoed the appeal, emphasizing reduced import spending amid Middle East conflict, leading to a sell-off in jewellery stocks.

title: US passenger evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests ‘mildly’ positive
content: According to the department, both passengers travelled in the aircraft’s biocontainment units “out of an abundance of caution.” Officials said the passengers are being transported to a specialised facility in Nebraska, while the individual showing mild symptoms will be shifted to a separate centre. Three passengers aboard the MV Hondius have died, while several others have fallen ill due to the rare rodent-borne disease.

title: 'I will establish my innocence': Vinesh Phogat responds to WFI show-cause notice
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat has formally responded to the Wrestling Federation of India's notice. She asserts her eligibility to return to competitive wrestling from January 1, 2026. Phogat claims she has official confirmation from the International Testing Agency. She intends to compete in the ongoing Senior Open Ranking Tournament. Phogat denies anti-doping rule violations and allegations regarding selection trials.

title: After 43 years, US family sold electrical company for $1.7B, gave workers $240M
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: AIADMK at breaking point? Revolt brews against EPS after Tamil Nadu poll debacle
content: AIADMK faces internal turmoil following a significant electoral defeat in Tamil Nadu, with divisions surfacing over leadership and potential support for the new TVK government. MLAs arrived in separate groups for the assembly, highlighting a visible rift and raising questions about the party's future cohesion and relevance.

title: TVK’s JCD Prabhakar set to become Tamil Nadu speaker unopposed as Vijay backs seasoned hand
content: TVK MLA J C D Prabhakar has filed his nomination for the Speaker of the Tamil Nadu assembly, poised for an unopposed election.  The 73-year-old, a former AIADMK member, brings significant legislative experience to the role, balancing the ruling combine's youthful image.

title: India’s growth story: Q4 FY26 GDP seen at healthy 7.2%, FY27 growth pegged at 6.6%
content: Despite global economic headwinds from Middle East turmoil, India's economy is projected for stable growth through FY26 and FY27, with SBI forecasting around 7.5% for FY26.  Resilient domestic demand, strong credit expansion, and broad-based improvements in high-frequency indicators support this optimistic outlook, though external vulnerabilities like oil prices and currency movements remain key concerns.

title: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink disagrees with everyone who says there is AI bubble
content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink refutes AI bubble concerns, citing a significant shortage in computing capacity, chips, and memory. He predicts a future where financial markets will trade futures of compute, highlighting the growing demand for AI infrastructure. BlackRock is actively investing in data centers and energy assets to meet this demand.

title: 'Make every effort to save fuel': Govt reiterates PM Modi's appeal, assures there is no crisis
content: India's government is urging citizens to cut petrol and diesel use. This comes as the Strait of Hormuz situation tightens and global oil prices climb. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed for reduced consumption. Public transport and electric vehicles are encouraged. Fuel supplies remain stable for consumers. However, state-owned oil companies face significant losses.

title: ‘Worth fighting for’: Krunal’s painful scar photo goes viral after RCB-MI thriller
content: Krunal Pandya battled severe cramps to score a match-winning 73 off 46 balls, powering Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a thrilling last-ball victory against his former franchise, Mumbai Indians. Despite immense physical pain, Pandya's resilience and crucial partnerships revived RCB's chase, securing a vital win that knocked MI out of playoff contention.

title: Kunal Kemmu catches thief at 4am; Soha Ali Khan says she cried in fear
content: Soha Ali Khan recounted a terrifying 2011 home invasion where her husband, Kunal Kemmu, confronted the intruder while she stood by, admitting her fear and desire to be braver. She also shared insights into her family dynamics, including seeking relationship advice from brother Saif Ali Khan and getting amusing updates from Kareena Kapoor Khan.

title: Time for Mumbai Indians to bench Suryakumar and Tilak ; rest Jasprit Bumrah
content: Mumbai Indians crashed out of the IPL after a loss to the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, marking their sixth consecutive season without a trophy. Inconsistent performances from the likes of Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma and a lack of support for Jasprit Bumrah have plagued the team. Yet, Head coach Mahela Jayawardene continues to back the 'core group' despite the struggles.

title: India should resist EU, US pressure on data exclusivity in farm-chemicals: GTRI flags
content: India faces pressure from the EU and US to adopt data exclusivity in trade deals, but a GTRI report warns this could harm its agrochemical industry. Such provisions would create extended monopolies, delay generic pesticide approvals, increase import reliance, and raise costs for farmers, potentially undermining a key export sector.

title: After Bengal poll win, BJP’s fish fest to reassure voters
content: Following their swearing-in, BJP MLAs Agnimitra Paul, Dilip Ghosh, and Tapas Roy organized a 'fish festival' across Kolkata to counter perceptions of the party's aversion to non-vegetarian food. The events, attended by numerous party workers and locals, featured traditional 'machh-bhat' lunches, aiming to reassure voters about preserving Bengali culinary identity.

title: ‘State Barbers of India’ to ‘kesh withdrawal’: SBI ATM salon sparks meme fest
content: An SBI ATM booth in Danapur, Bihar, has been repurposed into a hair salon, baffling unsuspecting customers. The kiosk retains its original exterior, leading many to enter expecting to withdraw cash, only to find haircuts in progress. The property owner reportedly rented the space after ATM operations ceased, with the tenant converting it into a barber shop.

title: 'Fear out, trust in': Suvendu Adhikari takes 6 big decisions as Bengal CM in first cabinet meet
content: West Bengal's new Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari chaired his first cabinet meeting, approving six key decisions to end state-Centre deadlock. These include facilitating land for BSF border fencing, rolling out central schemes like Ayushman Bharat, and aligning IAS/IPS officers with central training.

title: Vinesh reaches Gonda amid fresh WFI standoff, insists she is eligible to compete
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat asserts her eligibility for the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda, despite the Wrestling Federation of India's (WFI) stance. WFI claims she must complete disciplinary proceedings and adhere to a mandatory notice period for retired athletes. Phogat argues she informed United World Wrestling of her intent to resume competition, but the federation remains unconvinced.

title: The story behind Pokhran: How one 'peaceful explosion' in 1974 made India a nuclear power
content: India's nuclear journey began with the 1974 'Peaceful Nuclear Explosion' at Pokhran, making it the sixth nation to harness atomic power for defence. Driven by regional security concerns, India conducted the 'Operation Shakti' tests in 1998, solidifying its status as a nuclear power. These advancements underscore India's strategic capabilities and technological prowess.

title: UP horror: Fed up with son’s drug addiction, parents hire killer to eliminate him
content: Fed up with their 32-year-old son's drug addiction and abuse, parents and his younger brother allegedly hired a contract killer for Rs 5 lakh to murder him. The mother even sold her jewellery for an advance payment. The son was found strangled to death and his body dumped in a canal.

title: Transgender persons ineligible to avail surrogacy services: Centre
content: The Centre has clarified that transgender individuals, regardless of marital status, are ineligible for surrogacy services under the Surrogacy (Regulation) Act, 2021. This ruling came after Kerala inquired about a legally recognized transgender woman married to a man seeking surrogacy. The law currently restricts eligibility to specific categories of intending couples and women.

title: ‘Bring back Bhuvi!’ Ashwin makes big India T20I call after RCB star destroys MI
content: Ravichandran Ashwin advocates for Bhuvneshwar Kumar's return to India's T20I squad following his stellar all-round display for RCB. Kumar's exceptional bowling, and a crucial six in the final over, impressed Ashwin, who highlighted his ability to perform under pressure. Kumar currently leads the Purple Cap race, showcasing consistent wicket-taking prowess.

title: SIT quizzes state women’s panel ex-chief for 6 hours over links to Nashik astrologer
content: Former state women's commission chairperson Rupali Chakankar was questioned for six hours by a Special Investigation Team probing allegations against jailed astrologer Ashok Kharat. The SIT focused on her association with Kharat and his trust, with Chakankar stating she provided fitting answers. The inquiry is ongoing to determine the extent of her involvement.

title: UP man crushes mother's lover to death with brick, attends wedding after killing to dodge suspicion
content: Lucknow Police have arrested a 22-year-old man and detained his minor relative for the brutal murder of a 28-year-old sanitation worker. The accused allegedly killed the victim over his relationship with the accused's mother, a liaison that had reportedly lasted for three years. The police cracked the case within 24 hours by analyzing CCTV footage and electronic surveillance.

title: 1 dead, over 30 injured after bus overturns multiple times on Ganga Expressway
content: A sleeper bus overturned on the Ganga Expressway near Budaun after a tyre burst, resulting in one passenger's death and over 30 injuries. The driver lost control, causing the bus to flip multiple times before landing on the opposite lane. Six passengers remain in critical condition and have been referred for advanced treatment.

title: No plan to hike gold and silver import duties, US-India trade talks expected soon
content: Government sources indicate no immediate plans to hike gold and silver import duties, despite calls to curb non-essential gold purchases. Meanwhile, India anticipates US trade talks addressing pending issues, including a Section 301 probe, though a comprehensive deal remains distant. India has contested US allegations of structural overcapacity, urging the termination of the investigation.

title: Hezbollah's FPV drones, explained: Why Israel is struggling to counter them
content: Hezbollah's deployment of fibre-optic FPV drones along Israel's northern border presents a significant defence challenge. These small, low-flying drones bypass jamming systems, making them difficult to detect and intercept. Israel is racing to develop countermeasures against this evolving threat, which has already resulted in casualties and damage to military assets.

title: ‘Clothes torn, racist slurs hurled’: 2 northeast women allege assault outside Delhi club
content: Two women, one from Assam and another from Bihar, were allegedly assaulted and molested by a group of men in Nehru Place, Delhi. The incident, which began with derogatory remarks, escalated into a physical altercation where their clothes were torn. Police have registered a case and are conducting raids to apprehend four identified accused.

title: ‘One of them sharpshooter’: How a UPI toll payment led cops to Suvendu's PA's murder suspects
content: A digital toll payment cracked the murder of Chandranath Rath, aide to Suvendu Adhikari. Police arrested three suspects from Uttar Pradesh and Bihar. The investigation suggests eight people were involved. Rath was shot dead near his residence. The attackers used a car with fake number plates. Police are continuing raids to find the remaining accused.

title: Watch: Skies above Somnath come alive as IAF's Suryakiran aerobatic team performs stunning manoeuvres
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi participated in the Somnath Amrut Mahotsav, commemorating 75 years since the restored temple's inauguration. He offered prayers, performed rituals, and paid tribute to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. The event also featured aerial displays by the IAF's Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team.

title: Vaishnaw reiterates PM Modi’s call to cut gold purchases: 'Do whatever best you can'
content: Amidst the escalating Middle East conflict, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw echoed PM Modi's call for Indians to reduce import spending and conserve foreign exchange. He urged citizens and businesses to make conscious choices to save forex, highlighting fuel expenditure as a key area for reduction. This initiative aims to protect the nation's economic interests as global energy supplies face strain.

title: ‘I’ll play for my captain Ruturaj’: Samson breaks silence on CSK leadership buzz
content: Sanju Samson has clarified his commitment to Chennai Super Kings, emphasising his dedication to playing under captain Ruturaj Gaikwad. Despite trade speculation, Samson expressed his desire to contribute to the team and highlighted his strong bond with Gaikwad. He also shared his admiration for MS Dhoni, cherishing moments spent with the CSK legend.

title: Gadkari inaugurates multi lane free flow tolling in Delhi: How will it help commuters?
content: Delhi's Urban Extension Road-II now features Multi Lane Free Flow tolling. This technology allows vehicles to pass without stopping. Road Transport Minister Nitin Gadkari inaugurated the system. This upgrade aims to improve connectivity for nearby villages and habitations. It also supports smoother urban traffic flow. This barrier-less tolling system is a significant step in modernizing India's transport infrastructure.

title: "Tareekh Pe Tareekh": Why Allahabad High Court Invoked Sunny Deol's Movie Dialogue
content: Justice Deshwal observed that judicial officers in Uttar Pradesh are frustrated because they are unable to perform their duties due to insufficient staff, non-cooperation by the police, faulty investigations, and improper forensic reports

title: Why PM Asked Households To Cut Use Of Cooking Oil And What's The Alternative
content: India imported $19.5 billion worth of vegetable oils in 2025-26 -- a massive outflow of forex for something used daily in millions of kitchens.

title: A Year After Op Sindoor, Pak Army Chief's Unprovoked Threat To India
content: Reiterating his warning, Munir said that if "Pakistan's enemies" undertook any such move in the future, the effects of war would not remain limited.

title: 6 Killed, 4 Injuured As Overloaded Car Falls Into Gorge In Himachal's Chamba
content: The Toyota Innova vehicle, overloaded with 10 people onboard, was on its way from Manali to Dalhousie when the incident occurred on the Lahru-Tunnuhatti road.

title: Man Kills Wife, Pregnant Daughter, Son With Axe In Jharkhand Over Dispute
content: Senior Superintendent of Police (East Singhbhum) Piyush Pandey said the accused, Ravindra Prasad Singh, who retired from a steel company in February, had been involved in a prolonged family dispute.

title: Pak Minister's "Oridinary Worker" Of Asim Munir Remark Sparks Online Backlash
content: "When ministers begin introducing themselves as 'humble workers' of powerful men rather than representatives of the people, one has to ask -- is this democracy or the court of loyal servants?" wrote Fatima Malik on X.

title: Iran Hangs Man Convicted Of Spying, Leaking Secrets To Mossad, US
content: The Islamic Republic of Iran executed at least 1,500 people last year, one of the highest numbers worldwide, according to Iran Human Rights.

title: Iran War Could Make Trump's Trip To China A Bit Chillier Than His Last Visit
content: Donald Trump is expected to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday night and will take part in a welcome ceremony with Xi Jinping the next day.

title: PM Modi Calls For WFH, Travel Curbs Amid Oil Price Hike. What Other Nations Did
content: New Delhi is the latest among a growing number of Asian economies calling for lower energy consumption amid skyrocketing fuel prices due to Iran tensions.

title: Middle East Conflicts Pose A Danger To Whales Off South Africa: Study
content: Environmental scientists, activists and even social media posts have highlighted the dangers of maritime traffic for sea mammals.

title: "Family Table Was Left Empty": Hantavirus Renews Memories Of 2018 Outbreak
content: Human-to-human transmission of hantavirus was first discovered in 1996 in the a town near El Bolson, was later found to have happened in Epuyen.

title: Opinion: Opinion | What's Behind Araghchi's Visit To China Right Before Trump-Xi Meeting?
content: Beijing remains indispensable to Tehran as an economic backer and diplomatic shield, but it is not prepared to become its war ally.

title: Mother's Day Gift Ideas: Five Financial Plans To Give Her Lifelong Security
content: Mother's Day Gift Ideas: Financial planners and real estate experts say the Mother's Day gift need not be symbolic. It could be structural.

title: Bengaluru Businessman Downloads Wedding Invite On WhatsApp, Loses Rs 5 lakh
content: A man received an APK file along with the wedding invite text on WhatsApp. The APK file was allegedly used to install a mobile application.

title: New 10-Lane Delhi-Jewar Corridor Planned Ahead Of Noida Airport Launch
content: This route will start from Sector 94 and extend up to Gharbara village in Greater Noida, where it will merge with the Yamuna Expressway.

title: 'Stay calm, avoid panic': Rajnath Singh reviews energy supply amid Middle East crisis
content:
NEW DELHI: Defence minister Rajnath Singh on Monday chaired the 5th meeting of IGoM in Delhi to review the existing risks to energy supply chains and domestic availability of essential commodities in the wake of the conflict in West Asia. “I urge people to remain calm and avoid any kind of panic as the govt is taking concrete steps to prevent shortages or disruptions in supply chains," Rajnath said.

Watch

Why PM Modi Wants Indians To Avoid Gold Purchases And Foreign Travel

Also read: No duty hike on gold, silver as US-India trade talks near "Shri PM Modi’s appeal for collective participation of the people to help the country face global disruptions and challenges is going to be a significant step towards self-reliance and ensuring energy security. It is PM’s broader message about conservation during a difficult global period,” he added, while referring to the PM’s Sunday message to conserve energy and fuel.

End of Article
